results.title: C2C role | GCP AI Engineer | Irving, TX, or Charlotte (100% Onsite) | Corp to Corp
highlights:
C2C role | GCP AI Engineer | Irving, TX, or Charlotte (100% Onsite) | Corp to Corp Get all C2C Jobs / hotlists 🔥 Alerts
...
Role- GCP AI Engineer
...
Location- Irving, TX, or Charlotte (100% Onsite)
...
Job Summary:We are seeking an innovative and highly skilled AI Engineer to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will bridge the gap between traditional software engineering and cutting-edge artificial intelligence. You will be instrumental in designing, building, and deploying advanced AI agents, working closely with Large Language Models (LLMs), and driving automated code generation initiatives. If you have a strong foundation in Java and Python, coupled with hands-on experience using Google's AI tools, we want you to help us build the next generation of intelligent applications
...
- AI Agent Development: Design, build, and deploy autonomous AI agents capable of reasoning, planning, and executing complex workflows.
...
- LLM Integration: Integrate cutting-edge Large Language Models (LLMs) into our core products and services to enhance functionality and user experience.
...
- Model Context Protocol (MCP) Implementation: Utilize the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to securely connect our AI models to various data sources, tools, and development environments.
...
- Automated Code Generation: Leverage AI and LLMs to build systems that assist in, or fully automate, code generation, testing, and optimization processes.
...
- System Engineering: Write clean, scalable, and maintainable code in both Java and Python to support AI backend infrastructure.
...
- Google Ecosystem Integration: Utilize Google ADK (AI Developer Kits) and related Google Cloud AI services (e.g., Vertex AI, Gemini APIs) to deploy robust AI solutions.
...
- Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with product managers, data scientists, and frontend engineers to translate business requirements into technical AI solutions.
...
Must-Have Qualifications:
...
· Programming Languages: Strong proficiency in both Java and Python, with a proven track record of building production-grade software.
...
· Google AI Tools: Hands-on experience with Google ADK (or equivalent Google Cloud AI/Vertex AI tools).
...
· LLM Expertise: Deep comfort level and practical experience working with Large Language Models (prompt engineering, fine-tuning, RAG architectures).
...
· Agentic Workflows: Demonstrable experience in building and orchestrating AI Agents (using frameworks like LangChain, LangGraph, or custom implementations).
...
· MCP Knowledge: Familiarity and practical experience with the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for standardizing AI interactions with external tools.
...
· Code Generation: Experience in leveraging AI tools or building pipelines specifically for code generation and software automation.
...
· Experience with modern robust backend frameworks (e.g., Spring Boot for Java, FastAPI for Python).
...
· Familiarity with containerization and orchestration (Docker, Kubernetes).
...
· Experience with vector databases (e.g., Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus).

results.title: CIO interview: Damian Leach, Vistra | Technology News
highlights:
# CIO interview: Damian Leach, Vistra
...
With its customers using multiple financial professional services in its portfolio, corporate services provider Vistra wants to provide a single platform. The digital backbone will also give customers access to better quality business information through embedded artificial intelligence (AI).
...
Damian Leach, recently installed chief AI and digital officer at Vistra, has been tasked with accelerating the group’s capabilities in digitally enabled and AI-powered professional financial services. Vistra has around 10,000 staff across 65 global locations, and offers tax, payroll, fund and entity management services among others.
...
“We can play a pivotal role in cleaning
...
for customers within
...
under management, not only unifying the clean data that we have, but providing the ability to ask questions of that data to gain intelligent insights
...
Leach says that by infusing AI into the workflow behind the scenes, a modern approach to the customer journey is created. Vistra is building the platform itself and will use AI offered by hyperscalers, with orchestration and automation tools.
...
## AI opportunity
...
Leach tells Computer Weekly that AI is a huge opportunity for Vistra to improve its services despite some misgivings across wider industry.
...
“There’s a lot of judgement that comes about through the topic of AI, because a lot of people tend to think AI is about automating processes,” he says. “This is somewhat true, but I think the real benefit is improving the client experience. If you connect the client to the data that they have and put intelligent services over it to build those ‘what if?’ scenarios, they can question the data and we can open new services.”
...
In terms of its own IT resources, Vistra has 450 tech staff which all sit in Leach’s team. He describes his role as being like a CIO, chief technology officer (CTO) and chief data officer (CDO) rolled into one, heading up infrastructure core services such as datacentres, end user computing, as well as networks, voice and collaboration services and cloud.
...
Leach says one of the biggest challenges faced when adopting AI is the availability of the right skills, which are “the biggest challenge as we enter the AI era, and learning and focusing on talent and skills within the existing team”.
...
He says to address this he has three combined strategies. The first is to build and foster grassroots innovation in the existing workforce: “It’s important for us to lift the existing core team skills and expertise that we have and arm them with the right tools and support needed to succeed. This includes providing psychological safety for the team to experiment, innovate and even fail as they learn, but at least [they gain] progress.”
...
He says employees understand the challenges the company has but perhaps lack the tools to help solve them. “Through innovation grassroots mindset, we can encourage the best-in-class ideas to surface and for people to stay as they learn and continue to contribute,” he adds.
...
The current drive is to encourage the development of a community of builders by releasing AI tools to them to start coding to meet some needs that they have.
...
Leach says the company is also “ sprinting” using new talent: “I’ll be setting up strategic relationships with key universities and taking in fresh graduates. It’s a focused project, building new talent from the ground up. And of course, here in Singapore, there’s great opportunity and very supportive government initiatives to meet the national AI strategy.”

results.title: CIO interview: Damian Leach, Vistra - Cybernoz
highlights:
With its customers using multiple financial professional services in its portfolio, corporate services provider Vistra wants to provide a single platform. The digital backbone will also give customers access to better quality business information through embedded artificial intelligence (AI).
...
Damian Leach, recently installed chief AI and digital officer at Vistra, has been tasked with accelerating the group’s capabilities in digitally enabled and AI-powered professional financial services. Vistra has around 10,000 staff across 65 global locations, and offers tax, payroll, fund and entity management services among others.
...
One of the key benefits for Vistra’s customers, according to Leach, is providing them with better quality data about their business: “Data remains one of the largest issues for our customers. Often, organisations have fragmented data in silos, working from multiple sources, which leads to fragmented output and multiple answers to the same question.
...
“We can play a pivotal role in cleaning up that view for customers within their portfolios that we have under management, not only unifying the clean data that we have, but providing the ability to ask questions of that data to gain intelligent insights.”
...
Leach says that by infusing AI into the workflow behind the scenes, a modern approach to the customer journey is created. Vistra is building the platform itself and will use AI offered by hyperscalers, with orchestration and automation tools.
...
## AI opportunity
...
Leach tells Computer Weekly that AI is a huge opportunity for Vistra to improve its services despite some misgivings across wider industry.
...
“There’s a lot of judgement that comes about through the topic of AI, because a lot of people tend to think AI is about automating processes,” he says. “This is somewhat true, but I think the real benefit is improving the client experience. If you connect the client to the data that they have and put intelligent services over it to build those ‘what if?’ scenarios, they can question the data and we can open new services.”
...
In terms of its own IT resources, Vistra has 450 tech staff which all sit in Leach’s team. He describes his role as being like a CIO, chief technology officer (CTO) and chief data officer (CDO) rolled into one, heading up infrastructure core services such as datacentres, end user computing, as well as networks, voice and collaboration services and cloud.
...
Leach says one of the biggest challenges faced when adopting AI is the availability of the right skills, which are “the biggest challenge as we enter the AI era, and learning and focusing on talent and skills within the existing team”.
...
He says to address this he has three combined strategies. The first is to build and foster grassroots innovation in the existing workforce: “It’s important for us to lift the existing core team skills and expertise that we have and arm them with the right tools and support needed to succeed. This includes providing psychological safety for the team to experiment, innovate and even fail as they learn, but at least [they gain] progress.”
...
He says employees understand the challenges the company has but perhaps lack the tools to help solve them. “Through innovation grassroots mindset, we can encourage the best-in-class ideas to surface and for people to stay as they learn and continue to contribute,” he adds.
...
The current drive is to encourage the development of a community of builders by releasing AI tools to them to start coding to meet some needs that they have.
...
Leach says the company is also “sprinting” using new talent: “I’ll be setting up strategic relationships with key universities and taking in fresh graduates. It’s a focused project, building new talent from the ground up. And of course, here in Singapore, there’s great opportunity and very supportive government initiatives to meet the national AI strategy.”

results.title: LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
highlights:
LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
...
# LLM Engineer
...
Employer VIB Location Leuven Closing date 19 Aug 2026
...
VIB is a leading life sciences research institute in Flanders, Belgium, with a strong focus on translating basic scientific discoveries into benefits for society. With the launch of VIB.AI, we are expanding our mission to harness the power of artificial intelligence for life sciences research, innovation, and impact.
...
The VIB Center for Neuroscience Leuven (CNL) investigates the mechanisms of brain function and neurological disease across the scales of biology, spanning neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment, synaptic biology, and computational neuroscience. AI and large language models have transformative potential across these research areas.
...
We are now looking for an LLM Engineer in a shared position between VIB.AI's Machine Learning Expertise Unit and the Center for Neuroscience. You will split your time between building institute-wide AI tools and applying them directly to neuroscience research challenges.
...
As an LLM Engineer in this shared position, you will:
...
- Develop and deploy LLM-based pilot projects tailored to neuroscience research at the CNL, applying AI to questions in molecular, cellular and circuit neuroscience, neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment and disease mechanisms.
...
- Build and maintain components of VIB's institutional intelligence layer, including agentic bioinformatics workflows, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, and AI-assisted experimental design tools.
...
- Design, implement, and evaluate LLM-based solutions for scientific text mining, multimodal integration of literature and biological data, and knowledge extraction to support discovery.
...
- Collaborate with wet-lab and computational neuroscience researchers to identify opportunities where generative AI and LLMs can accelerate scientific progress.
...
- Work with the Machine Learning Expertise Unit and VIB's AI Studio to translate pilot projects into practical tools that serve the broader VIB community.
...
- Promote ethical, transparent, and responsible use of LLMs in line with best practices and international standards.
...
- Mine opportunities and data at the center’s advanced technology expertise centers and affiliated central core facilities.
...
- Leverage VIB's Data Core services, including high-performance computing pipelines and large-scale GPU resources, to scale LLM development and deployment.
...
- PhD in machine learning, computer science, computational biology, computational neuroscience, statistics, or a related field.
- Strong experience with large language models, including pretraining, fine-tuning, prompt engineering, and evaluation.
- Knowledge of modern deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow).
- Experience with real-world applications of LLMs in scientific or biomedical contexts is a plus.
...
- Familiarity with neuroscience data types (imaging, electrophysiology, genomics, proteomics, or clinical data) is a plus but not required.
...
- Ability to work collaboratively across disciplines and communicate effectively with researchers from different backgrounds.
- A strong interest in applying LLMs to impactful life science and neuroscience problems.
...
opportunity to pioneer applications of LLMs in neuroscience and the broader life sciences.
...
an AI platform team and a world-class neuroscience research center, with exposure to both engineering and scientific challenges.
...
- Direct access to VIB's Data Core services, which provide expert support in data management and high-performance computing, including optimized pipelines and large-scale GPU resources.
...
Please complete the application through the VIB online application tool. A complete application file should contain the following documents:
...
For further information, please send an email to stein.aerts@kuleuven.be or joost.schymkowitz@kuleuven.be with subject title Inquiry: LLM Engineer CNL/VIB.AI.

highlights:

# Postdoctoral Research Fellow - Computational Biology/Bioinformatics
...
Employer Visterra, Inc. Location Waltham, Massachusetts Start date Jul 2, 2026
...
Discipline Information Technology, Bioinformatics, Regulatory, Science/R&D, Biology Required Education Doctorate/PHD/MD Position Type Full time Hotbed Genetown, Best Places to Work
...
Postdoctoral Research Fellow – Computational Biology/Bioinformatics (Computational Single Cell and Genetics)
...
Visterra is seeking a highly talented, self-motivated, and innovative Postdoctoral Research Fellow to join our Genomics Platform team. This role offers a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of computational biology and therapeutic discovery, applying cutting-edge single-cell multi-omics and genetics tools to advance precision medicine. The fellow will conceptualize and implement advanced computational approaches for analyzing large-scale single-cell multi-omics and genetics datasets to derive novel biological insights.
...
disciplinary teams,
...
clinical translational applications
...
This temporary, full-time position is on-site at Visterra’s offices in Waltham, MA, with an expected duration of 2 years. The fellowship provides an excellent opportunity to gain valuable experience in computational biology at Visterra. Exceptional performers may be considered for long-term career opportunities within the company.
...
- Develop and implement advanced computational and machine learning strategies, including deep learning, graph-based methods, and probabilistic modeling, to extract insights from multi-omic single-cell and spatial datasets
...
- Integrate population-level genetic association data (e.g., GWAS summary statistics, eQTL resources) with single-cell and spatial datasets to contextualize cellular findings within a genetic framework.
...
omics/genetics data
...
novel therapeutic development
...
- Stay current with advancements in computational biology by reviewing literature, evaluating new technologies, and implementing relevant methodologies.
...
- D. or equivalent in computational biology, bioinformatics, or a related field.
...
- Experience with single-cell or spatial transcriptomics analysis.
...
- Expertise in genetics analysis (e.g., GWAS, eQTL, variant calling).
...
- Proficiency in computational tools such as Seurat, Scanpy, PLINK, LDSC and other relevant methods.
...
- Strong programming skills in Python and/or R for bioinformatics applications.
...
- Experience working in high-performance computing frameworks.
...
- Passion for developing computational technologies for biological research and translating findings into therapeutic applications in precision medicine and translational research.
...
Visterra is a team of 110 talented and driven individuals working collaboratively to develop a pipeline of innovative antibody-based therapies for patients with unmet needs. Our proprietary technology platform, powered by a state-of-the-art laboratory, enables the design and engineering of precision antibody-based therapeutics that selectively modulate key disease targets. Our focus includes kidney, immune, and other hard-to-treat diseases.
...
Visterra is a clinical stage biotechnology company committed to developing innovative antibody-based therapies for the treatment of patients with kidney diseases and other hard-to-treat diseases. Our proprietary technology platform enables the design and engineering of precision antibody-based product candidates that specifically bind to, and modulate, key disease targets. Applying this technology to disease targets that are not adequately addressed by traditional therapeutic approaches, we are developing a robust pipeline of novel therapies for patients with unmet needs.

results.title: Director, Enterprise AI Platform Architect | Jobflarely
highlights:
Director, Enterprise AI Platform Architect | Jobflarely
...
# Director, Enterprise AI Platform Architect
...
Vista Equity Partners is seeking a hands-on Enterprise AI Platform Architect to drive AI innovation across our 90+ portfolio companies. This role sits at the center of Vista’s Agentic Factory, our methodology for collaborating with portfolio companies to identify high-value agentic use cases and rapidly build production AI agents that deliver measurable business impact.
...
The ideal candidate combines deep technical expertise in generative AI, agents, and agentic architectures with the ability to move fast, shipping MVPs in compressed timelines and designing platforms that perform reliably in enterprise environments. You will work directly with portfolio company technology teams to design, implement, and scale AI solutions that enhance product offerings and drive competitive advantage.
...
- Design scalable, resilient, and secure AI platforms leveraging public cloud AI services (AWS, Azure, GCP) while maintaining vendor independence through thoughtful abstraction layers.
...
- Develop and maintain reference architectures for AI applications, including generative AI models, agentic systems, and multi-agent collaboration patterns.
...
- AI Innovation & Strategy
...
- Serve as a subject matter expert on generative AI, agents, and agentic architectures, providing thought leadership and strategic guidance to portfolio companies and Vista leadership.
...
- Identify and evaluate emerging AI technologies and trends, assessing their potential impact on portfolio company product roadmaps and Vista’s investment thesis.
...
- Technical Expertise
- Generative AI including LLMs, diffusion models, and RAG architectures
- Agentic architectures, autonomous agents, and multi-agent systems
- Cloud AI platforms across AWS, Azure, and GCP
- AI model development, deployment, and lifecycle management
- Prompt engineering and model fine-tuning
- Machine learning and deep learning fundamentals
- Platform abstraction and vendor-independent architecture design
...
- Qualifications:
...
- Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field.
...
- 3+ years ofexperience in AI/ML platform architecture and development, with deep recent experience (2+ years) in generative AI and agentic architectures in production applications.
...
- Demonstrated track record of shipping AI applications to production environments, not just prototypes.
...
- Strong understanding of public cloud AI services and the ability to architect vendor-agnostic solutions with appropriate abstraction layers.

results.title: Senior Data Scientist, AI/ML– Visa Consulting and Analytics | Hirevector
highlights:
Senior Data Scientist, AI/ML– Visa Consulting and Analytics | Hirevector
...
# Senior Data Scientist, AI/ML– Visa Consulting and Analytics
...
The Global Data Science group supports our bank and merchant partners by using our extraordinarily rich data sets that span more than 3 billion cards globally and captures more than 100 billion transactions in a single year. Our focus lies on building creative solutions that have an immediate impact on the business of our highly analytical partners. To support our rapidly growing scope of work capabilities at the intersection of data science, analytics and AI, we are looking for Senior Data Scientists who are passionate and leading team members and solving complex data problems using Visa rich dataset and technical toolkit. # Machine Learning (ML) Engineer at VIB.AI
...
Employer VIB Location Leuven Closing date 21 Aug 2026
...
## Job Details
...
Application deadline: August 31, 2026 About us VIB is a leading life sciences research institute in Flanders, Belgium, with a strong focus on translating basic scientific discoveries into benefits for society. With the launch of VIB.AI, we are expanding our mission to harness the power of artificial intelligence for life sciences research, innovation, and impact. We are now looking for a Machine Learning (ML) Engineer to join the Machine Learning Expertise Unit at VIB.AI. Your role As an ML Engineer, you will: Develop and deploy ML solutions in close collaboration with researchers across VIB centers, applying state-of-the-art methods to cutting-edge biomedical questions.Design, implement, and evaluate ML and foundation-model approaches, including LLM-based solutions, for scientific text mining, multimodal integration of literature and biological data, and knowledge extraction to support discovery.Collaborate with researchers to identify opportunities where generative AI can accelerate scientific progress.Work within the Machine Learning Expertise Unit and with VIB AI Studio to translate pilot projects into practical tools.Promote ethical, transparent, and responsible use of AI in line with best practices and international standards.Leverage VIB Data Core services, including high-performance computing pipelines and large-scale GPU resources, to scale ML development and deployment. Your profile PhD in machine learning, computer science, computational biology, statistics, or a related field.Knowledge of modern deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow).Experience developing AI applications for scientific or biomedical research, including LLM-based and agentic AI systems, is a plus.Ability to work collaboratively across disciplines and communicate effectively with researchers from different backgrounds.A strong interest in applying ML to impactful life science problems.We welcome applications from both early-career and experienced ML engineers. Our offer A stimulating, international, interdisciplinary, collaborative, and supportive work environment, which emphasizes diversity and inclusion.An environment that promotes personal development.The opportunity to pioneer applications of ML in biomedical sciences.Direct access to VIB’s Data Core services, which provide expert support in data management and high-performance computing, including optimized pipelines and large-scale GPU resources.A competitive salary and benefits package, with relocation support if needed.The chance to make a lasting impact on science, health, and society. About VIB.AI VIB.AI is the artificial intelligence initiative of VIB, designed to accelerate breakthroughs in life sciences by integrating advanced AI methods into research, discovery, and innovation. For more information, visit vib.ai/en. How to apply Please complete the application through the VIB online application tool (https://jobs.vib.be/apply/135914). A complete application file should contain the following documents: Your motivation letter stating career goals, past experience, and how these relate to the current position.Your CV.Contact information of three references. Application deadline: August 31st 2026. For further information, please send an email to pedro.goncalves@kuleuven.be, dmitry.kobak@ugent.be or stein.aerts@kuleuven.be with subject title Inquiry: ML Engineer.

# VP, Artificial Intelligence - Products & Services
...
About the Role & TeamIDC is seeking a transformative AI leader to define and execute our enterprise-wide AI strategy—embedding cutting-edge artificial intelligence across both internal operations and customer-facing products. This is a rare opportunity to shape how a global data and intelligence company harnesses AI, from agentic workflows and large language models to predictive analytics and AI-native product experiences. Reporting directly to the CTO, the VP of AI will lead a high-performing technical team, partner across business units, and drive measurable business impact through intelligent, responsible, and scalable AI solutions. The ideal candidate combines deep technical expertise in modern AI—including generative AI, LLMs, and agentic systems—with the strategic acumen to align AI investments to revenue growth and competitive differentiation. IDC sits at the intersection of proprietary data, expert human insight, and global technology reach—an ideal foundation for AI transformation. Key opportunity areas include: AI-augmented research & analyst productivity — deploying LLM-powered tools to accelerate data synthesis, report generation, and market forecasting. Intelligent data products — embedding predictive models and AI-driven insights directly into IDC's subscription and advisory offerings. Agentic content & marketing workflows — leveraging generative AI to personalize content, automate content pipelines, and optimize Foundry's MarTech capabilities. Enterprise AI infrastructure — building shared platforms, APIs, and MLOps practices that enable AI adoption at scale across all IDC business units. What You’ll Do
...
AI Strategy & Vision: Partner with the CEO, CTO, CIO, and business unit heads to define IDC's multi-year AI roadmap—spanning generative AI, agentic systems, predictive analytics, and AI-native product development. Technical Leadership: Architect and oversee IDC's AI platform and infrastructure, ensuring scalability, security, and alignment with enterprise data strategy. Product Delivery: Develop and execute IDC’s approach to agentic AI technology, thereby delivering the centerpiece of IDC Quanta, our cross-channel technology intelligence layer. Team Building: Recruit, develop, and retain a world-class AI team spanning ML engineers, data scientists, AI product managers, and MLOps specialists. Stakeholder Alignment: Translate complex AI capabilities into clear business value for executives and department heads; maintain transparent goals and resource planning. Ecosystem & Partnerships: Evaluate and manage AI technology partnerships, vendor relationships, and open-source contributions to accelerate IDC's AI capabilities. What You Bring

About the position Visa AI as a Service (VAIaS) operationalizes the delivery of AI and decision intelligence to ensure their ongoing business values. Built with composable AI capabilities, privacy-enhancing computation, and cloud-native platforms, VAIaS automates the updates to data, models, and applications. Combined with strong AI governance, VAIaS optimizes the performance, scalability, interpretability and reliability of AI models and services. If you want to be in the exciting payment and AI space, learn fast, and make big impacts, Visa AI as a Service is an ideal place for you! This position is for a Senior Consultant level engineer with solid development experience who will focus on creating new capabilities for Visa AI as a Service while maturing our code base and development processes. In this position, you are first a passionate and talented developer that can work in a dynamic environment as a member of Agile Scrum teams. Your strong technical leadership, problem-solving abilities, coding, testing and debugging skills is just a start. You must be dedicated to filling product backlog and delivering production-ready code. You must be willing to go beyond the routine and prepared to do a little bit of everything. You will be an integral part of the development team, sometimes investigating new requirements and design and at times refactoring existing functionality for performance and maintainability, but always working on ways to make us more efficient and provide better solutions to our end customers. The role is for a self-organized individual with knowledge of web application and web service development. The candidate will perform hands-on activities including design, documentation, development and test of new functionality. Candidate must be flexible and willing to switch tasks based on team's needs. Responsibilities • Collaborate with project team members (Product Managers, Architects, Analysts, Software Engineers, Project Managers, etc.) to ensure development and implementation of new data-driven business solutions • Drive development effort End-to-End for on-time delivery of high-quality solutions that conform to requirements, conform to the architectural vision and comply with all applicable standards • Collaborate with senior technical staff and PM to identify, document, plan contingency, track and manage risks and issues until all are resolved • Present technical solutions, capabilities, considerations, and features in business terms • Effectively communicate status, issues, and risks in a precise and timely manner Requirements • 8 or more years of relevant work experience with a Bachelor Degree or at least 5 years of experience with an Advanced Degree (e.g. Masters, MBA, JD, MD) or 2 years of work experience with a PhD Nice-to-haves • 9 or more years of relevant work experience with a Bachelor Degree or 7 or more relevant years of experience with an Advanced Degree (e.g. Masters, MBA, JD, MD) or 3 or more years of experience with a PhD • Experience with Kafka, Spark, Golang, Backend dev, K8s, DevOps • Exposure to areas like Machine Learning, Big Data, Distributed Systems or SRE or Java, or C/C++ • Experience in at least one of the following: Golang, Java, or C/C++ • Familiarity with web service standards and related patterns (REST, gRPC) • Experience implementing solutions for low-latency, distributed services using open standard technologies Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • 401 (k) • FSA/HSA • Life Insurance • Paid Time Off • Wellness Program Apply tot his job

results.title: Harsh v
highlights:
Bringing over a decade of experience building large-scale AI/ML, NLP, and LLM architectures across AWS, Azure, and GCP, I specialize in designing enterprise AI platforms that power automation and intelligent workflows. My work spans end-to-end model development, scalable infrastructure, and production-grade deployment of advanced LLM and multi-agent systems. I architect RAG pipelines using LangChain, Pinecone, FAISS, Weaviate, and HuggingFace embeddings to deliver high-accuracy retrieval and knowledge-grounded reasoning. I build multi-agent workflows with LangGraph and AutoGen, integrating FastAPI, Databricks, PySpark, and vector databases for automated decision-making. My NLP experience includes transformer-based models such as BERT and RoBERTa, along with spaCy and NLTK for classification, summarization, extraction, and conversational AI. I integrate enterprise LLM services from Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, and Vertex AI and deploy them through Docker, Kubernetes, and Terraform. My data engineering background includes PySpark, Delta Lake, Databricks, and Snowflake to prepare ML-ready datasets and streamline modeling pipelines. I automate MLOps and LLMOps workflows with MLflow, DVC, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, SageMaker Pipelines, and feature stores. I build secure LLM microservices using FastAPI, Uvicorn, Docker, IAM, API Gateway, and Kubernetes. I apply LoRA, QLoRA, PEFT, and PyTorch Lightning to fine-tune models efficiently on GPU environments. My experience extends to document intelligence with OpenCV, Tesseract, and Vision AI, along with cloud observability using Prometheus, Grafana, CloudWatch, and LangSmith. I integrate enterprise systems using REST APIs, gRPC, Salesforce, ServiceNow, and cloud messaging services. I also manage DevOps and cloud governance with Terraform, Helm, Docker, and secure multi-cloud standards. Across organizations, I lead AI architecture reviews and build engineering alignment through LucidChart models, Confluence documentation, and JIRA roadmaps.
...
Architected enterprise AI platforms across AWS, Azure, and GCP using Kubernetes, Docker, Terraform, and multi-cloud networking to support large-scale LLM and automation workloads. Designed cross-cloud LLM strategy leveraging Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, and Vertex AI for distributed inference. Built platform-wide vector search systems using Pinecone, FAISS, Weaviate, and custom embedding pipelines to power enterprise retrieval. Implemented ML lifecycle tracking and observability using MLflow, LangSmith, and internal monitoring systems. Designed multi-agent ecosystems using LangGraph, AutoGen, and intelligent routing engines to orchestrate complex workflows. Developed secure model-serving layers with API Gateway, Nginx, WAF, and IAM, ensuring enterprise-grade access control. Integrated Databricks and Snowflake as unified computation and data platforms for large-scale AI workloads. Automated CI/CD for AI services using GitHub Actions, Jenkins, and container registries, enabling rapid and reliable deployment pipelines. Designed monitoring stacks using Prometheus, Grafana, and OpenTelemetry to capture metrics, logs, and traces across clusters. Built feature store architectures with Feast, Snowflake, and PySpark pipelines to standardize feature access and lineage. Optimized GPU workloads with NVIDIA CUDA, PyTorch enhancements, and TensorRT acceleration. Managed LLM optimization workflows, including quantization, distillation, and efficient transformer variants for performance and cost reduction. Led enterprise AI planning using JIRA roadmaps, Confluence documentation, and architecture review sessions. Documented end-to-end AI architecture using UML diagrams, LucidChart models, and standardized design templates. Results include reducing LLM latency by 40%, improving system uptime to 99.9%, cutting deployment cycles from weeks to hours, improving vector search accuracy by 35%, and lowering cloud costs by 30% through Terraform governance and GPU optimization.
